tool_input.summary: No dedicated PDS catalog dataset for a pre-made “inverted channel / paleolake” map turned up in the search results. To test whether mapped fluvial (including inverted-channel) systems imply associated paleolakes, you typically combine (1) high-resolution imaging to trace channel terminations and delta/fan morphologies, (2) MOLA/HRSC topography to verify closed basins and potential lake levels (spill points), and (3) CRISM spectroscopy to check for lacustrine/aqueous mineral signatures. Candidate PDS datasets below support that workflow (PDS3 and PDS4 where found).

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX images provide regional-scale morphology for mapping inverted channels, tracing channel terminations into basins, and identifying candidate deltaic/lacustrine landforms that would support a paleolake interpretation.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC regional image products (and associated topographic products via HRSC processing pipelines) are useful for basin-scale geomorphic context around inverted channels (e.g., paleolake basins, shoreline-like terraces, deltas/fans).

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Gridded MOLA topography enables quantitative tests for paleolakes: closed-basin delineation, spill-point elevation mapping, watershed boundaries, and estimating possible paleolake levels/volumes where channels terminate.

candidates.title: MGS M MOLA 5 SHADR V1
candidates.reasoning: MOLA shaded relief products are quick-look context layers for locating topographic lows and basin boundaries relative to inverted channel networks before doing quantitative basin analyses.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-SHADR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Same MOLA shaded-relief concept as above; useful for rapid visual assessment of whether inverted channel systems drain into enclosed depressions consistent with paleolakes.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 MOLA topography collection (in IMG format) provides topographic inputs for basin/spill-point analysis to evaluate whether inverted channels terminate at potential paleolake levels.

candidates.title: MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format
candidates.reasoning: Browse-ready MOLA topography images help screen candidate paleolake basins associated with fluvial/inverted-channel systems before deeper quantitative analysis.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: CRISM targeted reduced data records support checking for aqueous/lacustrine mineralogies (e.g., phyllosilicates, sulfates, carbonates) in basins where inverted channels terminate—evidence that strengthens a paleolake interpretation.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM VRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 CRISM hyperspectral RDRs provide mineralogical constraints on candidate paleolake deposits associated with inverted channels (e.g., altered basin-floor sediments, deltaic deposits).

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the mapped fluvial systems indicate the presence of paleolakes associated with the inverted channels?

parts.args: {"summary":"No dedicated PDS catalog dataset for a pre-made “inverted channel / paleolake” map turned up in the search results. To test whether mapped fluvial (including inverted-channel) systems imply associated paleolakes, you typically combine (1) high-resolution imaging to trace channel terminations and delta/fan morphologies, (2) MOLA/HRSC topography to verify closed basins and potential lake levels (spill points), and (3) CRISM spectroscopy to check for lacustrine/aqueous mineral signatures. Candidate PDS datasets below support that workflow (PDS3 and PDS4 where found).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CTX-2-EDR-L0-V1.0","path":null,"title":"VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"img","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CTX images provide regional-scale morphology for mapping inverted channels, tracing channel terminations into basins, and identifying candidate deltaic/lacustrine landforms that would support a paleolake interpretation."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","path":null,"title":"MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0","mission":"Mars Express","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC regional image products (and associated topographic products via HRSC processing pipelines) are useful for basin-scale geomorphic context around inverted channels (e.g., paleolake basins, shoreline-like terraces, deltas/fans)."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Gridded MOLA topography enables quantitative tests for paleolakes: closed-basin delineation, spill-point elevation mapping, watershed boundaries, and estimating possible paleolake levels/volumes where channels terminate."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-SHADR-V1","path":null,"title":"MGS M MOLA 5 SHADR V1","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOLA shaded relief products are quick-look context layers for locating topographic lows and basin boundaries relative to inverted channel networks before doing quantitative basin analyses."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-SHADR-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-SHADR-V1.0","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Same MOLA shaded-relief concept as above; useful for rapid visual assessment of whether inverted channel systems drain into enclosed depressions consistent with paleolakes."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_data::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Data in IMG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"PDS4 MOLA topography collection (in IMG format) provides topographic inputs for basin/spill-point analysis to evaluate whether inverted channels terminate at potential paleolake levels."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mex_marsis_optim:topography_image::1.0","path":null,"title":"MOLA Topography Images in IMG and PNG Format","mission":"Mars Global Surveyor","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Browse-ready MOLA topography images help screen candidate paleolake basins associated with fluvial/inverted-channel systems before deeper quantitative analysis."},{"dataset_id":"MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"CRISM targeted reduced data records support checking for aqueous/lacustrine mineralogies (e.g., phyllosilicates, sulfates, carbonates) in basins where inverted channels terminate—evidence that strengthens a paleolake interpretation."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_vrdr::1.0","path":null,"title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM VRDR Bundle","mission":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"PDS4 CRISM hyperspectral RDRs provide mineralogical constraints on candidate paleolake deposits associated with inverted channels (e.g., altered basin-floor sediments, deltaic deposits)."}]}
